notes: v2.4.1 — Hot-fix for v2.4 init_db migration on legacy DBs. v2.4 hung the entrypoint pre-flight import for 50+ minutes on slow storage with multi-GB legacy DBs (Hyper-V Azure RHEL VM, 208K vulns) due to two compounding bugs: a single-transaction marker UPDATE that blocked SQLite checkpoints for the duration of a full table scan, and an O(N²) cursor thrash in the streaming threat-column backfill once the marker committed. Mac M-series hosts didn't see the hang because faster CPU hid the quadratic-scan behavior. Fixes: marker UPDATE chunked into LIMIT 5000 batched loop with per-batch commits (resumable across kills via the marker column itself); _backfill_threat_columns rewritten to use a qid worklist + indexed PK lookups instead of a long-running cursor across commits; entrypoint pre-flight stops redirecting stderr to /dev/null and a new _init_progress() helper writes migration phase markers visible in real time via docker logs. New regression test test_marker_update_chunked_is_resumable_across_kills proves a kill mid-loop preserves committed work and a restart picks up where it left off. See BUGS.md BUG-017 and CHANGELOG.md v2.4.1 for the full incident write-up. Recommended upgrade path on weak hardware with a multi-GB legacy DB: docker compose down -v + fresh Full Sync rather than waiting out the legacy migration.

steps.comment: Update dependency list FIRST so pip install works
Install dependencies BEFORE replacing code — prevents import errors on reload
Replace entire app/ directory with new code (deps already installed)
Update entrypoint with self-heal logic for next restart
Ensure entrypoint is executable after copy
Update the manifest itself for future updates
Save version SHA and restart Gunicorn workers
